name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
description: An initiative by the Government of India that provides up to ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments to all small and marginal landholding farmer families as minimum income support.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year, paid in three installments of ₹2,000 directly to bank accounts.

eligibility.additional: Must own cultivable landholdings in their name. Exclusion categories apply (e.g., institutional landholders, income-tax payers, retired pensioners receiving >₹10,000/month).

application_mode: Online self-registration or through local Common Service Centers (CSC)

name: Ayushman Bharat - PM Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: The largest health assurance scheme in the world, aiming to provide a health cover of ₹5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to over 12 crore poor and vulnerable families.
benefits: Cashless health cover of ₹5,00,000 per family per year for hospitalization expenses, including pre and post-hospitalization costs.

eligibility.additional: Identified through SECC (Socio-Economic Caste Census) 2011 database. Automatically covers families living in one-room Kutcha houses, landless households deriving major income from manual casual labor.

application_mode: Walk-in verification at any empanelled hospital or Ayushman Mitra center

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban & Gramin (PMAY)
description: A flagship mission of the Government of India that addresses the housing shortage among the EWS/LIG and MIG categories by providing interest subsidies on home loans or direct financial assistance for house construction.
benefits: Interest subsidy of up to 6.5% on home loans, or direct financial assistance up to ₹1.2 lakh (plains) / ₹1.3 lakh (hilly areas) for house construction.

eligibility.additional: The beneficiary family must not own a pucca (brick) house in any part of India in their name or in the name of any family member. Women ownership or co-ownership of the house is preferred.

documents: Affidavit stating the family does not own a pucca house
application_mode: Online registration through the PMAY portal or local municipality offices

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A pension scheme focusing on unorganized sector workers, offering a guaranteed minimum pension of ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 per month from the age of 60, based on their contributions.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum pension of ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹3,000, ₹4,000 or ₹5,000 per month after attaining 60 years of age.

eligibility.additional: Must have a savings bank account. Must not be a member of any statutory social security scheme (like EPF, EPS) and must not be an income tax payer.

application_mode: Visit the branch of your bank/post office where you hold a savings account and submit the APY enrollment form

name: Ladli Laxmi Yojana (2.0)
description: A state-level initiative (popularized in Madhya Pradesh and other states) to improve the educational and economic status of girl children, prevent child marriage, and promote positive attitudes towards girl birth.
benefits: A cumulative fund of ₹1.43 lakhs distributed at milestones: schooling scholarships (₹2k-₹6k) and ₹1 lakh lumpsum on completing 21 years without early marriage.

eligibility.additional: Parents must be permanent residents of the state. Parents must not be income tax payers. Application must be filed within one year of the girl's birth (with birth certificate).

documents: Parental sterilization certificate (if applicable for second child)
application_mode: Online submission through Anganwadi workers or the Ladli Laxmi Portal
